bhupendra-singh.bio:
Dr. Bhupendra Singh is serving as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science & Engineering, IIIT Pune. He is also heading the CSE department. He is a Computer Hacking Forensic Certificate (CHFIv12) certified professional. His area of interest includes digital forensics, Windows forensics, database forensics, and Cyber Security Technologies. His research contribution involves the development of custom tools for various Operating Systems and metadata forensics in the field of digital forensics for crime investigation.

He has published several research articles in peer-reviewed journals and book chapters. He serves on the Technical Program Committee member of many international conferences on Digital Forensics and Cyber Security. He has taught Cyber Security and Digital Forensics, Python programming, and Cloud Computing to undergraduate students. Singh holds a Ph.D. in Computer Science & Engineering from Defence Institute of Advanced Technology (DIAT) Pune and M.Tech. from Central University of Rajasthan in Computer Science and Engineering. Dr. Singh has successfully guided one Ph.D. student and has recently secured a research grant of INR 4.2 crore from the Cyber Security Division, MeitY, for a project entitled "Next Generation Data Carving and Preservation Techniques for Storage Drive Forensics", in collaboration with NIT Raipur.

sanjeev-sharma.bio:
Dr. Sanjeev Sharma is an Assistant Professor (Level 12) at the Indian Institute of Information Technology, Pune (IIIT Pune). He holds a Ph.D. in Information Technology from ABV-IIITM Gwalior, specializing in multi-robot path planning and nature-inspired algorithms. His research interests include Artificial Intelligence, Deep Learning, Robotics, and Generative AI.

With over 15 years of academic and research experience, Dr. Sharma has taught courses such as Operating Systems, Database Management Systems, and Deep Learning. He has published over 60 research papers in reputed SCI/SCIE-indexed journals and international conferences in domains such as medical imaging, agriculture, and autonomous systems. He also serves as a reviewer for prestigious journals including Swarm Intelligence (Springer) and the IEEE Sensors Journal.

In addition to teaching and research, Dr. Sharma has contributed significantly to academic administration in multiple roles, including Associate Dean, Senate Member, Assistant Registrar (Academics), and Examination Superintendent. He is also a Senate-nominated member of the Board of Governors at IIIT Pune.

nagendra-kushwaha.bio:
Dr. Nagendra Kushwaha is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering at the Indian Institute of Information Technology, Pune. He received his M.Tech and Ph.D. in the broad areas of VLSI Design and Digital Systems. His academic and research interests lie at the intersection of low-power SoC design, processor–peripheral integration, mixed-signal circuits, and design automation for emerging applications.

Research Interests:
• Low-power System-on-Chip (SoC) architecture and design
• Processor–peripheral integration (UART, GPIO, I2C, SRAM, EEPROM)
• Embedded processor-based platforms (VEGA ET1031)
• Mixed-signal and analog circuit design (comparators, ADCs, PMU)
• Power Management Units (PMU) for SoC
• Hardware accelerators and PE array integration

Sponsored Projects:
"Power and Space Optimization in Internet of Things (IoT) System-on-Chip (SoC)"
Sponsoring Agency: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
Project Outlay: RS. 81.5 Lakhs

anagha-uday-khiste.bio: Dr. Anagha Khiste is currently working as an assistant professor at Indian Institute of Information and Technology, Pune, she is an academician and researcher with expertise in Mathematics and its applications to engineering and sciences. She is actively involved in teaching, research, student mentoring and academic administration. Her research interests include Discrete Mathematics, Lattice Theory, Graph theory, Algebra, Combinatorics, Optimization Techniques. She has published research articles in reputed journals and regularly contributes to curriculum development, project supervision and scholarly activities. She qualified the CSIR-NET with an All India Rank of 102. She is a recipient of the “Padmashree Late N. S. Venkatesan Gold Medal” for Best Ph.D. Thesis (2014-15) from Savitribai Phule Pune University (SPPU), Pune. She has also been awarded the Junior Research Fellowship (2011-2013) and the Eklavya Scholarship during her postgraduate studies.

jatin-majithia.bio:
Dr. Jatin Majithia joined the Indian Institute of Information Technology Pune (IIIT Pune) as an Assistant Professor in February 2022 and is currently associated with the Department of Applied Science and Humanities.

Prior to joining IIIT Pune, he worked as a Programmer at Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) and later served as a Research Fellow at the Indian Institute of Science Education and Research (IISER) Pune. He also has over seven years of teaching experience as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mathematics at the College of Engineering Pune (COEP).

Dr. Majithia's primary research interests lie in **Commutative Algebra** and its interactions with related areas such as **Graph Theory** and **Lattice Theory**. In recent years, he has also developed research interests in **Fractional Differential Equations** and their applications. His work focuses on exploring algebraic structures and their connections with discrete mathematical frameworks.

He has contributed chapters to books designed for Engineering Mathematics students and has been actively involved in teaching, curriculum development, and academic outreach. He has delivered invited lectures and conducted workshops at various institutions on topics including Linear Algebra, Differential Equations, Graph Theory, and their applications.

Dr. Majithia welcomes motivated students interested in pursuing research projects, internships, or doctoral studies in Commutative Algebra, Graph Theory, Lattice Theory, and Fractional Differential Equations.

Dr. Chandrakant Guled is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Applied Science and Humanities at Indian Institute of Information Technology Pune, India. shrikant-salve.bio:
Dr. Shrikant Salve is a dedicated academician and researcher with over 20 years of association with higher education, research, and professional development in the field of Computer Science and Human-Computer Interaction. He is currently serving as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science & Engineering at the Indian Institute of Information Technology Pune, where he teaches and mentors students in advanced domains such as Deep Learning, Machine Learning, and HCI. He completed his Ph.D. from the Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati, focusing on intelligent interface design for reducing data entry errors in rural India. His academic journey began with strong foundations in Information Technology, followed by research-intensive work in usability and user- centered AI. Over the years, he has contributed extensively through journal publications, international conferences, and Springer book chapters. His research spans Augmented Reality, usable security, AI-driven educational systems, and rural technology interventions.

He has actively organized and contributed to national and international academic events, ideathons, hackathons, FDP’s, and workshops. Dr. Shrikant has also taken up significant administrative and leadership roles, including Vice Chair of IFIP Working Group 13.1 on HCI Education. As a mentor, he supervises Ph.D. scholars working in areas such as Explainable AI, deep learning, and AR-based educational frameworks. With two decades of commitment to teaching, research, and academic leadership, he continues to contribute meaningfully to the advancement of HCI and AI in India and globally.

sonam-maurya.bio:
Dr. Sonam Maurya is working as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science & Engineering, IIIT Pune. Her research area of interest includes Wireless Sensor Network (WSN), Internet of Things (IoT), Vehicular Adhoc Network (VANET), Mobile and Wireless Networks, Machine Learning, Deep Learning and Image Processing. Dr. Sonam hold Ph.D. in Computer Science & Engineering from PDPM- Indian Institute of Information Technology, Design & Manufacturing (IIITDM), Jabalpur in 2019. She completed M.Tech in Computer Science and Engineering from Madan Mohan Malviya University of Technology, Gorakhpur (2014) and did her B.Tech in Computer Science & Engineering from United Institute of Technology, Allahabad (2011). Based on her Ph.D. research, she got the opportunity to visit Japan under “JENESYS-2018 Science and Technology Exchange” program. She also received “Fellowship for Training of Young Scientist” award by M.P. Council of Science and Technology for presenting her PhD research contribution in the field of Precision Agriculture. Dr. Sonam has published 15+ research papers in various reputed SCIE Journals and International Conferences. She also published 2 Indian Patents for her contributions in the domain of IoT and 4 Indian Design Patents for designing a “Vykunthapalli” Digital Board Game. She worked on an International Collaborative Project entitled “AMICA- Activity Monitoring Healthcare Android App development” with Digiotouch Estonia.

priyank-jain.bio:
Priyank Jain is currently employed as an Assistant Professor at IIIT Pune. With over Eight years of experience in the field of teaching and research, he has garnered extensive knowledge and expertise. Dr. Priyank Jain holds a Ph.D. in the field of Data Privacy and Data Science demonstrating his dedication to advancing knowledge in this specialized area from NIT Bhopal.

His areas of specialization encompass various domains, such as Data Science, Data Privacy and security, and Machine Learning. Dr. Priyank Jain has contributed significantly to the academic community through his publications in prestigious International conferences, International journals, and National conferences.

Moreover, he is an esteemed member of HIMSS, emphasizing his active engagement and participation in professional networks. Additionally, he has obtained some Granted Patents from International and national institutions, further highlighting his achievements and recognition in the field.

dheeraj-dubey.bio: Dr. Dheeraj Dubey earned his doctorate from Motilal Nehru National Institute of Technology (MNNIT) Allahabad, where he developed a passion for cutting-edge communication technologies. He went on to serve at NIT Raipur, where he spent two academic years shaping young minds in the Electronics and Communication Engineering (ECE) and Information Technology (IT) departments. Before stepping into his current role as Assistant Professor at IIIT Pune, Dheeraj was a Research Scientist at IIT Bombay, contributing to a groundbreaking MEITY-funded project focused on "Next Generation Wireless Research and Standardization on 5G and Beyond" His work during this time pushed the boundaries of wireless communication. With a keen interest in 5G communication and beyond, Dheeraj's research spans machine learning optimization and the performance enhancement of satellite and terrestrial communication systems through Free Space Optics (FSO). His efforts have consistently aimed at unlocking the future of connectivity, merging innovation with practical solutions that shape the evolution of modern communication networks.

sumit-kumar-gupta.bio: Dr. Sumit Kumar Gupta is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science & Engineering at Indian Institute of Information Technology Pune, where he also serves as Assistant Registrar (Academics). He holds a Ph.D. in Computer Science & Engineering from Maulana Azad National Institute of Technology Bhopal. With over a decade of academic and research experience, Dr. Gupta has made significant contributions to the fields of Graph Mining, Community Detection, Machine Learning Security, and Deep Learning. He has authored several high-impact research publications in reputed SCIE journals, Scopus-indexed journals, and leading international conferences. He is also the author of a book on Data Structures and holds a granted Australian patent in the domain of IoT-based intelligent traffic systems. In addition, he has two Indian patents currently under review for grant, further reflecting his ongoing innovation and research contributions. Dr. Gupta has an outstanding academic track record, having qualified the GATE examination 14 times and UGC-NET examination twice, reflecting his strong foundational knowledge and consistency in academic excellence. A Senior Member of IEEE, he actively contributes to the research community as a reviewer, session chair, and jury member at various prestigious IEEE and international conferences. He has chaired technical sessions at leading conferences and continues to support scholarly activities through peer review and mentorship. He has played a key role in establishing the IEEE Student Branch at IIIT Pune and currently serves as the Branch Counselor, guiding students toward innovation, research excellence, and professional development. Dr. Gupta is deeply committed to teaching and mentorship, currently supervising multiple Ph.D., postgraduate, and undergraduate students working on advanced research problems.

kaptan-singh.bio:
Dr. Kaptan Singh is currently working as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science & Engineering at the Indian Institute of Information Technology, Pune, Maharashtra. He holds a Ph.D. in Computer Science & Engineering from the Maulana Azad National Institute of Technology, Bhopal (NIT Bhopal), which he earned in 2022. Dr. Singh completed his M.E. in Computer Science & Engineering at the Truba Institute of Engineering and Information Technology, Bhopal, in 2012, and his B.E. at the University Institute of Technology, Barkatullah University, Bhopal, in 2005.

Dr. Kaptan's areas of specialization include the Web of Things, Cyber Security, Algorithms, and Theoretical Computer Science. Dr. Singh has made significant contributions to the academic community through his publications in prestigious international conferences, international journals, and national conferences.

Dr. Kaptan's has over 14 years of academic experience, having served as an Assistant Professor at various institutes. Dr. Singh has published more than 20 Scopus-indexed research papers in reputable journals and conferences, as well as more than 30 research papers in peer-reviewed and UGC-approved journals. Additionally, he has served as a reviewer for numerous reputed international journals and conferences.

shubham-shukla.bio:
Dr. Shubham Shukla is an accomplished academic and researcher with a strong foundation in engineering and technology. He completed his B.Tech from Uttar Pradesh Technical University and furthered his studies with an M.Tech and PhD from the Department of Electronics at the Central University of Allahabad. With over 8 years of experience, Dr. Shukla has made significant contributions to the fields of Robotics, Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Cyber-Physical Systems.

He has held positions at various engineering institutions and has been invited to deliver guest lectures on Robotics at prestigious institutions such as NIT Srinagar, Delhi Technological University, IIIT Pune, and Christ University. He has published research paper in world reputed journals Nature, as well as conference proceedings and national patents.

Dr. Shubham has important collaborations, signing MoUs with companies and institutions like NOKIA and MNNIT Allahabad. He has played a pivotal role in establishing Bio Medical Labs and Centres of Robotics at his previous institutions. His commitment to advancing the field is further demonstrated by his organization of various national and international conferences, Faculty Development Programs (FDPs), and workshops on Robotics.

dipen-bepari.bio:
Dr. Dipen Bepari is currently working as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering at the Indian Institute of Information Technology, Pune, Maharashtra. He received his B.Tech degree in Electronics and Communication Engineering from Jalpaiguri Government Engineering College, West Bengal, and his M.Tech from the National Institute of Technology, Durgapur, in 2008 and 2010, respectively. He earned his Ph.D. in Wireless Communication from the Department of Electronics Engineering, IIT (ISM), Dhanbad, in 2017.

Dr. Bepari's areas of expertise include Cognitive Radio, Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access (NOMA), Intelligent Reflective Surfaces (IRS), Energy Harvesting, and 5G and beyond communications. He has made significant contributions to the academic and research communities, publishing in prestigious SCI journals such as IEEE Communications Surveys & Tutorials and international conferences. Dr. Bepari has collaborated with globally recognized researchers and served as a reviewer for esteemed journals, including the IEEE Internet of Things Journal, IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology, and IEEE Communications Letters.

Google Scholar: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=_lxJQiMAAAAJ&hl=en

shivangi-shukla.bio: Dr. Shivangi Shukla is currently working as Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at the Indian Institute of Information Technology (IIIT) Pune. Dr. Shivangi's has received her Ph.D. in Computer Engineering from Sardar Vallabhbhai National Institute of Technology, Surat in 2024. She completed her M.Tech from Department of Computer Engineering, Sardar Vallabhbhai National Institute of Technology, Surat in 2018 and her B.Tech from Computer Science and Engineering, Dr. A. P. J. Abdul Kalam Technical University, Uttar Pradesh in 2015. Dr. Shivangi's area of specialization includes Information Security and Privacy, Cloud Computing Security and Security and Privacy in Internet of Things. She has published research papers in various international journals and conferences.

km-divya-chaturvedi.bio:
Dr. Divya is currently working as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering at the Indian Institute of Information Technology, Pune, Maharashtra. She received her B. Tech degree in Electronics and Communication Engineering from Uttar Pradesh Technical University, Lucknow, and her M. Tech from Pondicherry Central University, in 2011 and 2015, respectively. She earned her Ph.D. degree in RF/Microwave Engineering and from the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering, NIT Trichy in 2019.

Dr. Divya's areas of expertise include Substrate Integrated Waveguide (SIW) based Cavity-backed Multi-band Antennas with Intrinsically Isolated Ports, Antenna-Array Sensors for breast Cancer Detection, Smart Antenna Design with IoT enabled devices, Beamforming Antenna array for 5G applications, Fluid based antenna for 6G communication. She has been cited in top 2% scientist declared by Stanford University, 2023. She has received multiple projects as Principal Investigator from various reputed agencies like SERB, CSIR, ISRO. She has received young scientist award from DST to present her work in international conferences APMC 2018 at Japan, EuCAP 2019 at Poland, and PIERS 2023 at Czech Republic. She has received Best Researcher award in the Experimental category from SRM University-AP with Emoluments of 50K. She has also made significant contributions to the academic and research communities, publishing in prestigious IEEE transactions /Letters, and other SCI journals. Dr. Divya has served as TPC member for different conferences such as MAPCON, APMC, also invited to present her work in women in Engineering at MAPCON 2023, Bangalore. Currently, she is an active and senior member of IEEE and WiE.

habila-basumatary.bio: Dr. Habila Basumatary is currently working as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at Indian Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Pune, Maharashtra. Prior to that she has worked as an Institute Post-Doctoral Fellow (IPDF) in the department of Computer Science and Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology, Guwahati. She received her Ph.D. degree from the Computer Science and Engineering (CSE) Department, National Institute of Technology (NIT), Agartala, India in 2022. Dr. Basumatary completed her M.Tech and B.Tech in Information Technology (IT) from North Eastern Regional Institute of Science and Technology (NERIST), Arunachal Pradesh and Assam University Silchar Assam in 2017 and 2015 respectively.Dr. Habila's area of specialization includes Machine/Deep Learning, Cyber Security in IoT, Smart Micro-Grids, EV Charging. She has published numerous research papers in various international journals and conferences. She also serves as a reviewer for various reputed international journals.

sanga-chaki.bio:
Dr. Sanga Chaki is currently working as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at Indian Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Pune, Maharashtra. Prior to that she has worked as an Assistant Professor in the department of Computer Science and Engineering, ITER, Siksha O Anusandhan, Bhubaneswar. She received her Ph.D. degree from the Indian Institute of Technology, Kharagpur in 2023. Dr. Chaki completed her M.E. and B.E. in Information Technology (IT) from Bengal Engineering and Science University Shibpur (IIEST), and West Bengal University of Technology in 2012 and 2010 respectively.

Dr. Sanga's areas of interest include Explainable Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Affective Computing, Sound and Music Computing, and Trustworthy AI.

mahesh-joshi.bio:
Dr. Mahesh Joshi is currently working as an Assistant Professor in the department of Computer Science and Engineering at the Indian Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Pune, Maharashtra. Prior to that he has worked as an Assistant Professor in the department of Computer Science and Engineering at Dr Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University Pune, Vignan’s Foundation for Science Technology and Research University Guntur, and St. Vincent Pallotti College of Engg. and Tech. (Autonomous) Nagpur. He has received Ph.D. degree from the department of Computer Science and Engineering (CSE) at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT), Indore, India in 2022. He has completed M.Tech (CSE) and B.E. (IT) from Visveswaraya National Institute of Technology (VNIT) Nagpur, Maharashtra and Sant Gadge Baba Amravati University, Amravati, Maharashtra, respectively.

Dr. Mahesh's area of specialization includes biometric authentication, partial fingerprint identification, biometric security, Internet-of-Things (IoT) security. His research papers are published in reputed international journals, books, and conference. He serves as a reviewer for renowned international journals and international conferences.

mohammad-akhlaqur-rahman.bio:
Dr Mohammad Akhlaqur Rahman is an Adjunct Assistant Professor in the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering at the Indian Institute of Information Technology Pune. Before joining the Indian Institute of Information Technology Pune, he worked as a Teaching Assistant in the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering at SVNIT Surat, Gujarat. He has Completed his PhD from the Department of Electrical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Patna in Medical Image Processing. He completed his B. Tech in Electronics Engineering and M. Tech in Communication and Information Systems from Aligarh Muslim University, Aligarh. His research interest includes Medical Signal and Image Processing. He is interested in developing Image processing and Deep Learning based algorithms for the detection, segmentation, and classification of different types of abnormalities present in various types of radiological and pathological slide images.

ashish-singh.bio: Dr. Ashish Singh is currently working as an Adjunct Assistant Professor in the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering at the Indian Institute of Information Technology, Pune, Maharashtra. Before joining IIIT Pune, he had worked as a Temporary Faculty at Punjab Engineering College (PEC), Chandigarh. Dr. Ashish hold Ph.D. in Low Power VLSI Interconnects from NIT Hamirpur, H.P. He completed his M.Tech in VLSI Design from Thapar University, Patiala and did his B.Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering from U.P.T.U., Lucknow, respectively. His research area of interest includes Ultra Low Power and high-speed VLSI interconnects, Carbon nanotube (CNTs), copper-CNT modeling, Through-silicon vias (TSVs), and through-glass via (TGV). He has several research publications in reputed international journals and conferences. He serves as a reviewer for renowned international journals and conferences.

yashdeep-singh.bio:
Mr. Yashdeep Singh is currently working as an Adjunct Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at the Indian Institute of Information Technology (IIIT) Pune, India. He completed his M.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati and is currently completing his Ph.D. at IIT Guwahati, where his thesis has been submitted and the defense is awaited.

His research focuses on preference-based matching, popular matching, approximation algorithms, and structural graph theory. He has published in international journals and conferences, including Concurrency and Computation: Practice and Experience (CCPE), IWOCA, and CANDAR. His work addresses hardness results and approximation guarantees for generalized matching models.

Yashdeep has two years of experience as an Assistant Professor at Jaypee University of Information Technology, where he taught undergraduate courses such as Algorithms, Theory of Computation, Database Management Systems, and Algorithm Design. He also served as a Teaching Assistant at IIT Guwahati during his M.Tech. and Ph.D. His interests include algorithm design, combinatorial optimization, and fair allocation mechanisms.

tushar-prakash-gawande.bio:
Dr. Tushar Prakash Gawande is currently working as an Adjunct Assistant Professor in the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering at the Indian Institute of Information Technology, Pune, Maharashtra. Before joining IIIT Pune, he had worked as a Contractual Faculty at Ramdeobaba University (RBU), Nagpur, Maharashtra. Dr. Tushar hold Ph.D. in VLSI and Nanotechnology from NIT Nagpur, Maharashtra. He completed his M.Tech in Micro-nano Electronics from IIITDM Jabalpur and did his B.Tech in Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering from Dr. B.A.M.U. Aurangabad, Maharashtra, respectively. My research interested includes Embedded system design, Analog circuit and layout design, Data analysis and Machine learning.

tushar-prakash-gawande.projects:
Ph.D. Thesis title- Electronic Nose System: Focus on gas quantification & drift

correction algorithms using Machine learning techniques. This work is

carried out under the supervision of Dr. Sharvari Deshmukh and Dr.

Raghavendra Deshmukh at VNIT Nagpur.

Project 1-In this work, MEMS based Electronic nose was developed for

measurement and monitoring of prominent industrial pollutants and

proposed a drift correction. A drift correction algorithm was developed

using experimental design and robust regression to correct the drift induced

in the sensors. The experiments consisted of eight experimental sets with

mixtures of H S, CO, NH and C H OH measured on the same sensor array

2 3 2 5

at the start and after a gap of 1 year. The methodology consisted of mapping

the response of sensors after 1 year compared with that observed at the very

start (day 1) of the experiment.

Project 2-In this work, an Electronic nose in conjunction with gas

analyzers is used to evaluate the odor performance of different parts of the

city. Five different locations were selected from city. Further co-relation

between the e-nose and gas analyzers was done with partial least-square

(PLS) regression. The analysis of variance result shows that the e-nose is

able to predict the result of gas analyzer for NH , H S, CO, and CH4.

M.Tech. Thesis title-Design and Analysis of low power LNA with high linearity for

wideband application. This work is carried out under the supervision of Dr.

P.N. Kondekar at IIITDM Jabalpur.

In this research, a Low Noise Amplifier (LNA) is designed for the

frequency range of 8.1-21GHz using CMOS 0.18µm technology. It is a

two-stage amplifier which first stage consist of common gate topology and

the second stage consist of cascode stage. The layout of LNA is done with

DRC and LVS clean and also parasitic extraction is done for post layout

simulation. The chip area of proposed LNA was 0.11mm2 and it consumes

power of 5.4mW from V =1V.

manish-kumar.bio:
Mr. Manish Kumar is currently working as an Adjunct Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at the Indian Institute of Information Technology Pune. Before joining IIIT Pune, he was working at Chaitanya Bharathi Institute of Technology, Hyderabad. He has submitted his Ph.D. thesis at Pondicherry University, a Central University, under the guidance of Dr. R. Subramanian. During his Ph.D., he was a JRF-qualified research scholar.

His doctoral research focuses on traffic sign detection for autonomous vehicles, where he applies graph algorithms to store and access data in cloud-based intelligent transportation systems efficiently. He completed his MTech. in Computer Science and Engineering from Pondicherry University and his B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering with distinction from Aryabhatta Knowledge University, Patna.

His research interests include Autonomous Driving Systems, Traffic Sign Detection, Graph Algorithms, Cloud Computing, Machine Learning, Deep Learning, and Computer Vision.
